name: ExtensionMessageHeader_Source_Endpoint
title: R5: Actual source address or Endpoint resource additional types
description: R5: `MessageHeader.source.endpoint[x]` additional types (Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Endpoint)) additional types from child elements (display, identifier, reference)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `MessageHeader.source.endpoint[x]` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`MessageHeader.source.endpoint[x]` 0..1 `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Endpoint), url`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `MessageHeader.source.endpoint[x]` 0..1 `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Endpoint), url`
*  R4B: `MessageHeader.source.endpoint` 1..1 `url`
*  R4: `MessageHeader.source.endpoint` 1..1 `url`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `MessageHeader.source.endpoint[x]` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `MessageHeader.source.endpoint` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The mappings for `MessageHeader.source.endpoint[x]` do not cover the following types: Reference.
The mappings for `MessageHeader.source.endpoint[x]` do not cover the following types based on type expansion: display, identifier, reference.

element.comment:
Element `MessageHeader.source.endpoint[x]` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `MessageHeader.source.endpoint` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The mappings for `MessageHeader.source.endpoint[x]` do not cover the following types: Reference.
The mappings for `MessageHeader.source.endpoint[x]` do not cover the following types based on type expansion: display, identifier, reference.
The url may be a non-resolvable URI for systems that do not use standard network-based addresses.

element.short: Actual source address or Endpoint resource
element.definition: Identifies the routing target to send acknowledgements to.
element.comment: The url may be a non-resolvable URI for systems that do not use standard network-based addresses.
